entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop (Pennisetum glaucum) being studied for resistance to downy mildew

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: A devastating disease in pearl millet caused by the pathogen Sclerospora graminicola

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes downy mildew in pearl millet

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: The genetic trait that allows pearl millet to withstand downy mildew infection

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE SCREENING
entities.description: An artificial screening method used to detect resistance to downy mildew globally

entities.name: QTL MAPPING
entities.description: The process of determining the genomic locations of quantitative trait loci for resistance

entities.name: FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: The natural environment used to identify resistance QTLs in India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where both field and glasshouse screens were conducted

entities.name: UK
entities.description: A country where glasshouse screens were conducted

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKER MAPPING
entities.description: The process of mapping markers in an F2 population to identify QTLs

entities.name: RESTRICTION FRAGMENT LENGTH POLYMORPHISMS
entities.description: The specific molecular markers used in the mapping process

entities.name: DISEASE INCIDENCE
entities.description: The phenotypic measure used to assess resistance in F4 families

entities.name: COMPOSITE INTERVAL MAPPING
entities.description: A statistical method used to detect associations between marker genotypes and family means

entities.name: MULTIPLE-TRAIT INTERVAL MAPPING
entities.description: A mapping method that provides increased power over single trait mapping to detect QTLs

entities.name: QTL X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The phenomenon where the magnitude of QTL effects varies across different screening environments
